prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is in black and white and appears to be staged like a studio presentation.

At the start, a curtained stage is shown. A spotlight falls on a small sign mounted on an easel. The sign reads “DON WILSON presents Derusto” or very similar; the product name “Derusto” is prominently centered. The background is a full curtain, giving the opening a theatrical or presenter-style setup.

The scene then cuts to a close-up of a metal can being held in someone’s hand. A second hand points toward the label. The can label prominently shows the brand name “Derusto,” with additional visible text including “Mint-Finish,” “dries in minutes,” and claims such as “prevents rust” and “stops rust.” The pointing gesture visually directs attention to the product name and benefits on the label.

Next, the camera shows an older man in a suit and tie standing in front of the curtain. He holds the can at chest height and points to it, continuing the demonstration-style presentation. The curtain backdrop remains visible, and the lighting creates a strong shadow behind him.

Later, the commercial cuts to a retail or promotional display. A large sign at the back reads “RUST PREVENTION HEADQUARTERS.” It features a smiling woman holding or presenting products, with images of the Derusto can and related product illustrations around her. In front of the sign is a long row of Derusto cans arranged in an orderly display, suggesting store availability or a product lineup.

The final frame returns to a close-up product shot. A single Derusto can sits on a plain surface, well lit and centered. The label is again clearly visible, emphasizing “Mint-Finish Derusto,” “dries in minutes,” and “prevents rust—stops rust,” along with “24 beautiful colors.” The commercial ends visually with the product isolated as the focal point.
